situation: A public health emergency has occurred, and you must prioritize messaging for different audiences based on their proximity to the event.

options.action: Prioritize safety and action messages for first responders, survivors, and local public health/medical communities first.
options.consequence: Ensures immediate safety and reduces harm for those most affected.

options.action: Send generic messages to all audiences simultaneously to avoid confusion.
options.consequence: May overwhelm or misinform audiences with varying needs, leading to panic or inaction.

options.action: Focus messaging on media and international audiences to control the narrative.
options.consequence: Neglects critical safety needs of those closest to the emergency, increasing risk.

common_rookie_mistake: Sending the same message to all audiences without segmenting based on proximity or needs.
consequence_if_wrong: Audiences may not receive critical safety information in time, leading to increased harm or panic.
rationale: Audiences closest to the emergency require immediate, actionable information to ensure safety. Segmenting messages ensures relevance and reduces confusion.

situation: Public trust in your organization has been damaged, and you must communicate to rebuild credibility and trust.

options.action: Release accurate information quickly and demonstrate empathy in messaging.
options.consequence: Rebuilds trust by showing accountability and concern for affected stakeholders.

options.action: Delay communication until all facts are confirmed to avoid mistakes.
options.consequence: Delays may allow misinformation to spread, further eroding trust.

options.action: Focus on controlling the narrative rather than addressing stakeholder concerns.
options.consequence: May appear dismissive, worsening reputational damage.

common_rookie_mistake: Waiting too long to communicate to ensure accuracy, which allows misinformation to spread.
consequence_if_wrong: Loss of public trust and credibility, making future crisis management more difficult.
rationale: Speed and accuracy in communication are critical to rebuilding trust. Empathy and openness address stakeholder concerns directly.
